energy.headline: Solar installs surged 205% before the tax credit cut – and reshaped the market
energy.summary: US homeowners rushed to install solar before federal tax credits expired, driving a historic 205% surge in demand and marking a shift toward integrated home electrification systems.

energy.headline: New US battery capacity in 2026: 24.3 GW of new battery storage to come online
energy.summary: 24.3 GW of utility-scale battery storage is expected to come online in 2026, surpassing 2025's 15 GW record, with 48% co-located with solar arrays to address grid reliability.

energy.headline: Batteries become the new home solar as net metering evolves and energy prices soar
energy.summary: As net metering rules change and electricity prices climb, homeowners are increasingly turning to battery storage to capture and use their own solar energy rather than exporting to the grid.

energy.headline: Net metering for solar owners suffers defeat in California courts
energy.summary: California appeals court upheld a 2022 regulatory decision reducing rooftop solar payment rates, siding with utilities and deepening concerns about the state's energy affordability crisis.

energy.headline: New York City finally gets its first home battery storage system
energy.summary: NYC installed its first residential battery storage system in Chinatown after nearly eight years of regulatory navigation, opening the door for home batteries across all five boroughs.

energy.headline: Why reforming rooftop solar and battery permitting belongs on every state affordability agenda
energy.summary: Permitting delays add $6,000-$7,000 to solar costs, causing one in five projects to be canceled; states are cutting red tape to accelerate installations and reduce prices.

energy.headline: Massachusetts Expands Solar Net Metering in 2025
energy.summary: Massachusetts expanded net metering cap from 10 kW to 25 kW AC, enabling homeowners to install larger systems and plan for EVs and heat pumps while receiving full retail-rate credits.

energy.headline: HNU Energy launches HiVE Gen 2 Home Battery with expanded storage capacity
energy.summary: Hawaii-based HNU Energy introduced the HiVE Gen 2 Battery offering up to 45 kWh of residential storage in a stackable design for full household backup and off-grid applications.

energy.headline: Rooftop solar could meet 40% of EU's long-term electricity demand
energy.summary: EU analysis shows rooftop solar on 271 million buildings could supply 40% of Europe's electricity by 2050, with current technology providing a practical path to climate goals.

energy.headline: Battery technology outlook for 2026 sharpens beyond lithium-ion
energy.summary: Long-duration storage, safety-driven procurement, and supply chain resilience are accelerating interest in non-lithium battery chemistries even as lithium-ion remains dominant.

energy.headline: Missed the 30% Tax Credit? How to Make Solar Work for You in 2026
energy.summary: Following federal tax credit expiration, homeowners must focus on state incentives, PPAs, and leasing options; battery storage and smart technologies become critical for maximizing returns.

energy.headline: Rooftop solar is booming, but not in the communities that need it most
energy.summary: Despite rising installations, rooftop solar deployment is concentrated in high-income neighborhoods; lower-income areas have 14.7% higher carbon offset potential but 47% fewer installations.

energy.headline: Home Battery Storage in 2026 Costs Sizes Incentives Guide
energy.summary: Federal 30% battery tax credit expired December 31, 2025; battery costs average $1,128/kWh but increasingly make economic sense with time-of-use rates and solar pairing.

energy.headline: US residential solar enters post-incentive era after ITC expiry surge
energy.summary: Following the One Big Beautiful Bill's elimination of the 30% residential tax credit, the US solar market is shifting toward third-party ownership models and integrated electrification.

energy.headline: Battery storage projects surge as utilities prepare for next grid era in 2026
energy.summary: Battery energy storage is rapidly emerging from niche to mainstream grid infrastructure, with deployment accelerating to meet renewable integration and grid reliability needs.

energy.headline: Almost overnight, the US is on way to having an oversupply of ESS battery cells
energy.summary: EV battery manufacturers are pivoting to energy storage production, with US domestic cell capacity sufficient to meet 100% of demand by 2026.

energy.headline: 2026 Federal Solar Incentives: What's Changing and How It Affects You
energy.summary: Residential solar tax credits expired December 31, 2025; third-party ownership via leases and PPAs remains eligible through 2028, with state incentives becoming increasingly important.

energy.headline: Your Guide to Home Batteries in 2026
energy.summary: A 13.5 kWh battery costs approximately $15,228 before incentives; financial viability depends on time-of-use rates, solar output, and local incentive programs.

energy.headline: A path to fast, cheap home solar and batteries: Go through the meter
energy.summary: Meter socket adapters from Tesla and others reduce soft costs by standardizing installations, cutting days of labor and thousands in costs for home solar and battery projects.

energy.headline: Solar incentives in 2026: How states are filling the gap after federal credit expires
energy.summary: SREC programs, production rebates, and smart tariffs in states like New Jersey, Illinois, and Massachusetts offer alternatives after federal tax credit expiration.

energy.headline: Net metering policies see significant revision in states across the US
energy.summary: About one-third of states are offering alternatives to traditional net metering or making significant revisions, with Nevada, Arizona, and other states modifying compensation structures.

energy.headline: How Do Rooftop Solar and EVs Affect Electricity Costs for Everyone Else?
energy.summary: Research shows rooftop solar adoption decreased utility revenue by 2.35%, leading to 1.48% customer rate increases, with disproportionate impact on low-income non-solar households.

energy.headline: Kingspan Completes 881-kW Rooftop Solar Installation at Illinois Manufacturing Facility
energy.summary: Kingspan's Illinois manufacturing facility completed an 881 kW rooftop solar system expected to generate 1 million kWh annually, offsetting 700 metric tons of carbon dioxide.

energy.headline: DIY Plug-in Solar Panels Gain Traction as States Advance Legislation
energy.summary: Six states are advancing legislation to allow DIY plug-in solar panels as homeowners seek affordable solutions amid energy affordability pressures.

energy.headline: Home Electrification Market Shifts to Integrated Energy Systems Post-Incentive
energy.summary: Following federal incentive expiration, homeowners increasingly plan coordinated solar, storage, EV charging, and heat pump systems for resilience and cost control rather than single upgrades.

v2x.headline: Rivian and Redwood Deploy Grid-Scale Second-Life EV Battery Storage System
v2x.summary: Rivian partnered with Redwood to deploy 10 MWh grid-scale battery storage using 100 second-life Rivian batteries at its Illinois facility, reducing costs and peak demand strain while demonstrating a scalable solution for reusing EV batteries in energy systems.

v2x.headline: Electric Vehicles as Distributed Grid Battery: Novel V2G Pilot Project Achieves Thousands in Annual Earnings
v2x.summary: A Delaware V2G pilot project demonstrates EV owners could earn up to $3,359 annually by allowing parked vehicles to function as distributed batteries, storing surplus electricity and discharging during peak demand periods.

v2x.headline: Volkswagen Launches Commercial V2G Offering in Germany from Q4 2026
v2x.summary: Volkswagen and subsidiary Elli announced a fully integrated V2G offer for German customers starting Q4 2026, enabling EV owners to integrate vehicles into the energy system and generate additional revenue through bidirectional charging.

v2x.headline: Puget Sound Energy Launches Washington State's First V2H Bidirectional Charging Pilot
v2x.summary: Puget Sound Energy began testing V2H technology in partnership with Ford, Kia, Wallbox, and ChargeScape, with participating EV owners providing backup home power while supporting grid reliability during peak demand periods.

v2x.headline: Grid Upgrades Should Precede V2G Deployment for Cost Efficiency, Study Finds
v2x.summary: Research from University of Michigan and international collaborators published in Joule found that upgrading grid infrastructure first before deploying V2G chargers is more cost-effective, with each charger needing to generate only $0.12-$0.18 daily versus $1.49-$1.78 under phased approaches.

v2x.headline: Nissan Launches Affordable AC-Based V2G Technology in 2026
v2x.summary: Nissan announced it will launch affordable bidirectional charging on selected electric vehicles in 2026 after gaining G99 Grid code certification with AC-based solutions, aiming to make V2G accessible to more people through integrated on-board chargers.

v2x.headline: Enphase Unveils IQ Bidirectional EV Charger for V2H and V2G Services
v2x.summary: Enphase Energy unveiled its IQ Bidirectional EV Charger with AI-powered energy management for home backup and grid services, expected to be available in H2 2026 with simple installation requiring only a meter collar for basic configuration.

v2x.headline: 2026: Bidirectional Charging Breakthrough Year with Mainstream EV Adoption
v2x.summary: EcoFlow analysis shows 2026 as a breakthrough year for bidirectional charging, with major automakers including Ford, GM, Tesla, Mitsubishi, and Volvo integrating V2L, V2H, and V2G capabilities across their lineups.

v2x.headline: V2H Could Save EV Owners Thousands Over Vehicle Lifetime
v2x.summary: University of Michigan study finds V2H charging could save owners $2,400-$5,600 over 15 years and reduce emissions by 70-250%, with some regions seeing cost savings exceeding the total vehicle charging cost.

v2x.headline: GM Energy Becomes Leading V2H EV Provider with 246,000+ Units Equipped
v2x.summary: General Motors sold more than 246,000 V2H-capable EVs through its Ultium platform, leading the market, and is exploring leasing options for V2H systems to lower upfront costs and expand accessibility.

v2x.headline: V2H Technology Launches for Mass Market with Kia EV9 and Wallbox Collaboration
v2x.summary: UC Irvine and Kia America deployed six homes with mass-market V2H technology using Kia EV9 and Wallbox Quasar 2 charger, marking the first demonstration of commercial V2H products in real residential settings.

v2x.headline: EV Batteries Could Meet Grid Storage Demand by 2030, Study Shows
v2x.summary: Nature Communications research indicates EV batteries alone could satisfy short-term grid storage demand as early as 2030 with higher participation rates, with potential reaching 68-144 TWh by 2050.

v2x.headline: Utilities Deploy Managed Charging Programs to Handle Growing EV Load
v2x.summary: U.S. utilities are developing managed charging programs with software providers and automakers to spread EV charging load and avoid peak demand stress, though lacking standardized data-sharing protocols hampers adoption.

v2x.headline: Bidirectional Charging EV Act Introduced to Accelerate V2G and V2H Deployment
v2x.summary: Congresswoman Julia Brownley reintroduced legislation requiring all new EVs manufactured by 2029 to support bidirectional charging and directing DOE to develop a National EV Bidirectional Charging Roadmap.

v2x.headline: Ford, Nissan, and Volvo Lead V2H and V2G Adoption Among Established Manufacturers
v2x.summary: Analysis shows Ford F-150 Lightning with mature V2G technology, Nissan Leaf with stable V2G performance, and Volvo EX90 launching V2G services in North America in 2026 as market leaders in bidirectional charging.

v2x.headline: Australian Standards Now Approve V2G and V2H Bidirectional Charging
v2x.summary: Australia's latest electrical standards (AS/NZS 4777.2:2020 and AS/NZS 4777.1:2024) now allow bidirectional charging, enabling V2G and V2H technology deployment with costs typically ranging from $5,000 to over $10,000.

v2x.headline: Bidirectional EV Charging as Standard: 2026 Marks Mainstream Market Shift
v2x.summary: Analysis shows 54 EV models now support V2L with only 2 official V2H/V2G capable vehicles as of February 2026, with majority of new 2026 EVs providing some bidirectional capability, making it a breakthrough year for the technology.

v2x.headline: V2H Systems Require Standardized Protocols for Multi-Manufacturer Compatibility
v2x.summary: Industry moving toward ISO 15118-20 standard for bidirectional communication over NACS connectors to allow chargers and vehicles from different manufacturers to work together, though proprietary systems currently dominate the U.S. market.

v2x.headline: Bidirectional Charging Could Generate $22 Billion in Annual European System Cost Savings by 2040
v2x.summary: Research indicates V2G could save up to €22 billion annually in European system costs by 2040, with bidirectional charging becoming critical for grid stability and renewable energy integration.

v2x.headline: Scientific Study Demonstrates ANN-Controlled Bidirectional EV Charger with Solar Integration Achieves 90%+ Efficiency
v2x.summary: Research published in Scientific Reports shows an artificial neural network-controlled bidirectional EV charger integrated with solar PV achieves over 90% charging efficiency while supporting both Grid-to-Vehicle and Vehicle-to-Home operating modes.

v2x.headline: Only 12 EVs Currently Support Official V2H Home Backup Capability in U.S.
v2x.summary: Recharged's 2026 guide shows only about a dozen EVs officially support V2H today, with systems costing $4,000-$10,000 installed, though more models expected as third-party hardware becomes less expensive.

v2x.headline: V2H Expansion Across GM Ultium Platform Vehicles Accelerates Energy Independence
v2x.summary: GM's Ultium-based EVs including Cadillac Lyriq, Chevy Silverado EV, and GMC Sierra EV now offer V2H capability, with GM Energy exploring lease programs to reduce upfront costs and increase adoption.

v2x.headline: Virtual Power Plants Enable Large-Scale V2G Deployment Through Vehicle Aggregation
v2x.summary: Utilities increasingly embracing virtual power plant aggregation of distributed energy resources including V2G-capable EVs, with studies projecting EV batteries could provide 32-62 TWh capacity by 2050.

v2x.headline: EV Grid Integration Challenges Require Standardized Data-Sharing Protocols and Regional Regulations
v2x.summary: Grid integration studies show EV charging impacts on transformers and voltage stability require comprehensive mitigation strategies, with renewable energy integration and smart charging being critical for distribution grid stability.
